completedWork: LC Filter Design Simulation - NEW: Interactive LC filter designer with real-time Bode plot frequency response visualization. Filter type selection: low-pass (inductor in series, capacitor in shunt) and high-pass (capacitor in series, inductor in shunt). Component controls: inductance (1µH-100mH), capacitance (100pF-1000µF). Real-time calculations: cutoff frequency f_c = 1/(2π√LC), Q factor, characteristic impedance Z_0 = √(L/C), damping ratio ζ = 1/(2Q). Frequency response curve with logarithmic frequency axis (10Hz-100kHz) and gain axis (-20dB to +20dB). Cutoff frequency marker at -3dB point. Interactive circuit diagram with animated component symbols and labels. Quick presets: audio crossover (1kHz), RF IF filter (455kHz), power supply filter (100Hz), RF frontend (10MHz). Educational content: LC filter theory, filter types comparison, Q factor guide (Q<0.5 overdamped, Q=0.5 critically damped, Q=0.707 Butterworth, Q>0.707 underdamped with peaking), design equations, common applications (audio crossovers, power supplies, RF circuits, communication systems, signal processing), and pro tips (high-Q components, source/load impedance effects, parasitic considerations at high frequencies, cascading for steeper filters).

Voltage Regulator Design Calculator - NEW: Comprehensive voltage regulator design tool supporting LDO (linear), Buck (step-down), and Boost (step-up) topologies. Input parameters: input voltage (1-100V), output voltage (0.8-50V), output current (0.01-50A). LDO-specific: dropout voltage (0.1-5V), ground pin current (1-10mA). Switching regulator-specific: switching frequency (10kHz-5MHz), expected efficiency (70-98%). Real-time calculations: power dissipation, efficiency (η = V_out/V_in × 100% for LDO), input power, output power, input current, power loss percentage, temperature rise (assuming SOT-223 package, 60°C/W thermal resistance). Component recommendations: LDO chip examples (AMS1117, LM317, LT1085), Buck converter ICs (MP1584, LM2596, TPS5430), Boost converter ICs (MT3608, LM2577, LT1370). Inductor calculations: L_min based on ripple current (Buck: L = V_out×(1-D)/(f_sw×ΔI_L), Boost: L = V_in×D/(f_sw×ΔI_L)), saturation current rating, DCR requirements. Output capacitor calculations with ESR requirements. Feedback resistor values for adjustable regulators (assuming 0.8V reference). Dropout voltage warning for LDO when V_in - V_out < V_do. Quick presets: USB 5V (7-12V→5V@2A, Buck), MCU 3.3V (5V→3.3V@500mA, LDO), LED 12V (20-24V→12V@3A, Buck), LiPo Boost (3.7V→5V@1A, Boost). Educational content: LDO vs switching regulator comparison (advantages/disadvantages), key equations per topology, thermal management (T_J = T_A + P_loss×R_θJA, package thermal resistances), design tips (low-ESR capacitors, component placement, star grounding, LC filtering for noise), and common pitfalls (insufficient headroom, undersized inductors, poor capacitor selection, inadequate thermal management).
